summary: The cricothyroid muscle receives additional innervation via an extra pathway involving another cranial nerve component besides just the recurrent laryngeal nerve; thus making its supply unique among those listed here. Agent1 concludes based on this distinction between single vs multiple pathways supplying specific vocal cord functions which makes clear why only one option fits criteria given initially posed scenario regarding exclusivity within these choices presented concerning their sole reliance upon RLN versus mixed/alternative sources too.
